commit.message:
fix: add pyte and pillow to Docker for terminal_inspector tool

Delegated agents need terminal_inspector for shell commands, which requires
pyte (terminal emulation) and pillow (screenshots). Without pyte, agents
cannot run bash commands and the dev machine stalls.

commit.message:
fix(hot-reload): handle TypeLoadException from stale FSI compilation units

detourMethod now catches TargetInvocationException wrapping TypeLoadException
and TypeInitializationException from MonoMod when detouring methods from stale
FSI compilation units (FSI_0020 etc). Previously this propagated as
'SageFsInternal error occurred' and killed the eval. Now it logs at debug level
and skips the detour — the new definition supersedes the old one anyway.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix Claude tool calling: merge multi-choice API responses

The Copilot API returns Claude tool calls split across separate choices
(choices[0]=text, choices[1]=tool_calls) unlike OpenAI which combines
them in a single choice. Added normalizer in call_copilot() to merge
multi-choice responses into one unified message.

Also removed Claude from _NO_TOOL_CHOICE_MODELS since tool_choice=auto
works correctly through the Copilot API proxy.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: bootstrap dev dependencies after volume mount in Docker

Root cause: docker-run.sh mounts host project to /workspace, which shadows
the build-time .venv. Pyright reads from .venv (configured in pyproject.toml),
not system Python, so pip install amplifier-core was invisible to pyright.

Fix: docker-entrypoint.sh runs `uv sync --extra dev` after the volume mount,
before amplifier starts. This recreates .venv with all deps in the mounted dir.

Changes:
- New: docker-entrypoint.sh (bootstrap script)
- Dockerfile: use entrypoint wrapper
- iteration.yaml: use `uv run` for tests/pyright consistently
- STATE.yaml: update blocker status

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Voice branch: modular agents, Claude tool-calling fix, LearnNewAgent

- Fix Claude multi-choice tool calling (Copilot API splits text/tools into separate choices)
- Add system_context() hook to BasicAgent for modular prompt injection
- Move memory instructions from brainstem into ContextMemory agent
- Remove all hardcoded agent names from brainstem.py
- Add LearnNewAgent for runtime agent generation via Copilot API
- Fix test isolation bug (TestAgentLoading was overwriting real memory.json)
- Add .vscode/settings.json to prevent Live Server reload on data writes
- Enrich ManageMemory tool description for better Claude tool invocation
- Add ElevenLabs/Azure voice config endpoints with AES-encrypted zip storage
- Fix frontend API auto-detection for Live Server vs brainstem-served

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(acp): allow container document processing tools in restricted mode

Add 20 new allowed commands (wget, file, tree, jq, bc, pdftotext,
pdfinfo, pdfimages, pdftoppm, pandoc, exiftool, ffmpeg, ffprobe,
magick, convert, identify, mogrify, unzip, zip, 7zz) and 10 explicit
deny rules (git, python, python3, pip, pip3, gcc, g++, make, strace,
mkdir) to both OpenCode and Gemini agent restricted mode configs.

This enables Agent to use pre-installed container tools for document
processing during Self-Research (Feature 16) without requiring YOLO
mode. Only Layer 2 (agent config files) is modified; Layer 3-5
security controls remain unchanged.

Refs #288

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Signed-off-by: Jim Chen <Jim@ChenJ.im>

test: fix parallel test flakiness and bash 3.2 empty-array expansion

- Fix 'bats_opts[@]: unbound variable' under set -u + bash 3.2: empty
  arrays must use ${arr[@]+"${arr[@]}"} idiom, not "${arr[@]}"
- Split core_performance.bats out of the parallel batch; run it after
  all parallel workers finish so wall-clock timing assertions aren't
  skewed by CPU contention from concurrent bats processes
- Raise MOLE_PERF_GET_INVOKING_USER_LIMIT_MS default 500→2000ms and
  add MOLE_PERF_SECTION_LIMIT_MS (default 2000ms) to give sufficient
  headroom without masking real regressions

commit.message:
fix: use 'amplifier recipe execute' not 'amplifier run' in Dockerfile

Root cause: ENTRYPOINT was `amplifier run` but docker-run.sh passes
a recipe file (.dev-machine/build.yaml). `amplifier run` expects
bundles with session.orchestrator; recipes need `amplifier recipe execute`.

The error "Configuration must specify session.orchestrator" was correct -
`amplifier run` was trying to parse the recipe as a bundle.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: 再接続時に PTY 出力をリプレイする機能を追加

セッションごとに最大 100KB の出力リングバッファを保持し、
WebSocket 再接続時にバッファ内容を replay メッセージとして送信する。

- SessionManager に outputBuffer, appendOutput(), getOutputBuffer() を追加
- PTY onData で出力をバッファに蓄積
- 新規 WS 接続時に type:'replay' メッセージを送信
- クライアント側で replay 受信時に term.reset() → write で復元
- バッファ関連のユニットテスト 4 件追加

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(acp): remove invalid opencode.json permission patterns and update docs

Remove 9 broken permission patterns in opencode.json that relied on
unsupported env var expansion ($AGENT_WORKSPACE, $TMPDIR) and invalid
relative paths. OpenCode's wildcard engine only expands ~ and $HOME.

Changes:
- Remove broken env var patterns from edit and external_directory sections
- Remove broken relative path patterns (data/agent-workspace/**)
- Add /app/data/workspaces/*/tmp/** as absolute path replacement for TMPDIR
- Expand Pattern Matching Rules docs with regex-based engine details
- Add Known Limitations entry for OpenCode pattern matching
- Fix task permission discrepancy in docs table (deny, not allow)

Resolves #284, #285, #286

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Signed-off-by: Jim Chen <Jim@ChenJ.im>

commit.message:
feat(devreload): add post-patch canary validation to DevReloadInjector

Add the same canary validation used in HotReloading.fs to the
DevReloadInjector's patchMethod. Before applying a Harmony prefix
patch, snapshots the target method's JIT code bytes, then validates
them after patching to confirm the patch took effect.

For Harmony prefix patches, BytesUnchanged is logged as a warning
(not a degradation) since prefix hooks work differently from
MonoMod detours and may not alter native code bytes directly.

Includes 6 new tests in DevReloadCanaryTests.fs validating the
canary building blocks work correctly with Harmony prefix patches
on instance methods (matching DevReloadInjector's WebApplication
Run/RunAsync patching pattern).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add analysis pipeline builder and improve indicator blocks

Pipeline builder:
- AnalysisPipelineBuilder with fluent API to select indicators
- Parallel fan-out of candles to all indicators via Task.WhenAll
- EnrichedCandle model preserving original candle with indicator values
- Eager duplicate-name validation at Add() time
- Builder is safely reusable across multiple RunAsync calls
- Faulted indicators surface as AggregateException with context

Indicator improvements:
- Replace ContinueWith with async PropagateCompletionAsync helper
- SMA: store only Close values instead of full Candle references
- HMA: circular buffers eliminate per-candle array allocations
- Median: binary-search sorted insertion replaces full O(n log n) sort

Integration test:
- Pipeline test fetching 2 months of live MSFT data with all indicators

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: OpenQuant.NET
repository.description: OpenQuant.NET is an open‑source .NET library providing unified financial data access, quantitative analysis tools, and extensible market‑data integrations.

commit.message:
fix: move YAML frontmatter to line 1 in shadow-test-bundle.md

Amplifier requires frontmatter to start on line 1 (---).
Having a markdown heading before --- caused the entire session
config (including session.orchestrator) to be silently ignored,
producing 'Configuration must specify session.orchestrator'.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(coverage-intel): add bitmap fallback for unmapped symbols (P4)

Implement two-tier fallback in findCorrelatedTests:
- Tier 1: Check SymbolToTests when TransitiveCoverage has no entry
- Tier 2: findNearbyBitmapCoverage queries IL bitmaps for tests
  covering ±10 lines from the symbol location

Wire bitmap fallback into composeForFailure using median sequence
point line in causal files as the search center.

Add 15 new tests covering all fallback tiers, boundary conditions
(±10 line window), exclusion of self, and composition integration.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(supervisor): send bridge heartbeat before terminal for fast workers

Workers that complete without emitting heartbeat events left the lease
in STARTING state. The terminal heartbeat then failed because
STARTING → COMPLETING is not a valid transition (only RUNNING/HEARTBEATING
→ COMPLETING is allowed).

Now the supervisor sends a non-terminal bridge heartbeat before the
terminal one. This advances the lease STARTING → RUNNING, then the
terminal heartbeat completes RUNNING → COMPLETING. When the lease is
already past STARTING (from regular stream heartbeats), the bridge
heartbeat is handled as a normal heartbeat progression.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
chore: add rollback-release GitHub Actions workflow (#154)

* chore: add rollback-release GitHub Actions workflow

Manual workflow_dispatch that rolls back the VPS to any previously
released image version. Accepts a version string (e.g. 0.12.4 or v0.12.4),
strips the leading 'v' to match the Docker image tag convention, pulls
the pinned image on the VPS, retags it as :latest, and restarts via
docker compose — without touching .env or docker-compose.yml.

Shares the deploy-vps concurrency group to prevent concurrent
deploy/rollback races. Guarded to main branch only.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Potential fix for pull request finding

Co-authored-by: Copilot Autofix powered by AI <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Potential fix for pull request finding

Co-authored-by: Copilot Autofix powered by AI <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: add deploy-vps concurrency group to _deploy-vps.yml

The rollback workflow used group: deploy-vps but no existing deploy
workflow shared that group, so concurrent deploy + rollback runs were
not actually blocked.

Adding the same concurrency group to _deploy-vps.yml (the reusable
workflow called by both deploy.yml and release.yml) means all VPS
deployments now share the group with rollback.yml, enforcing real
mutual exclusion.

Addresses PR review comment: concurrency group not shared with existing deploy workflows

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot Autofix powered by AI <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(supervisor): transition lease LEASED → STARTING before heartbeat loop

The worker supervisor now transitions the lease from LEASED to STARTING
after spawning the worker process, before the heartbeat streaming loop
begins. Previously the lease stayed in LEASED state and heartbeats were
rejected because the heartbeat service only accepts STARTING, RUNNING,
or HEARTBEATING states.

Adds LeaseTransitionerPort to WorkerSupervisorDependencies (optional,
for backward compatibility) and wires the TransitionService adapter
in the automation runtime.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix infinite Resume or stop loop in unexpected state recovery (#37)

* Fix infinite 'Resume or stop' loop in unexpected state recovery

The fallback handler returned ask_user without setting CurrentState to
AwaitingUser, so user_chose events looped back to the same fallback.
Also handle push_completed from Investigating state for agents that
skip the investigation_complete -> apply_fix flow.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Capture prior state before mutating in recovery question

The question string was reading state.CurrentState after it was already
set to AwaitingUser, so the user would see the wrong state name.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add 'stop' choice handler to ProcessUserChoice terminal switch

The ChoiceValueMap mapped 'Stop monitoring' to 'stop' but ProcessUserChoice
had no case for it, falling through to unrecognized-choice. Also added a
test for the recovery-then-stop path.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Clear flow states in unexpected state recovery

Without clearing CommentFlow/CiFailureFlow, the recovery resume/stop
choices would route through flow-specific handlers instead of the
terminal-level switch, preventing stop from actually stopping.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix: backend fails to start; posts expire unexpectedly (#66)

* Initial plan

* fix: repair broken main.go and add configurable post TTL (fixes post expiration bug)

- Fix main.go: remove duplicate/conflicting code blocks that prevented the
  server from compiling. Use database.NewDatabaseFromURL (DB interface) with
  retry logic; single clean app.Listen call.
- mongo.go: add postTTLDays() helper reading POST_TTL_DAYS env var; InitSchema
  now only creates a MongoDB TTL index when POST_TTL_DAYS > 0, so posts
  persist forever by default. Log TTL status on startup.
- .env.template: document MONGODB_URI (takes precedence over DB_URL) and
  POST_TTL_DAYS (default 0 = no expiration).
- Add tests: mongo_test.go covers postTTLDays edge cases; main_test.go covers
  getEnv, resolveDatabaseURL, and retry helpers.

Co-authored-by: Bboy9090 <182185694+Bboy9090@users.noreply.github.com>
